title: Level 411
wikitext:
{{Rewrite|date=June 28, 2026|1=This page generally needs a makeover, both in formatting,  writing, an execution,  since currently it falls flat}}
{{Background|file=Warehousehallltestimage209.jpg}}
{{Image Banner|image=Levelbusbusbusbus209sandbox.jpg|caption=Routes are still marked, numbers are still assigned, but to where?|style=}}{{Alt Class|ent=2|noname=411|no=411|env=3|ext=2}}<center>


<big>"L411 Bus Depot"</big>
</center>

Upon entering '''Level 411''', one will find themselves within a bus depot, filled with an unusually high amounts of buses parked in rows, stretching far beyond visibility. The sun in this level never sets, causing the atmosphere in this level to be extremely hot and humid. Wanderers have reported fainting from the high temperature. Therefore, it is advised to bring any form of water or fluids for hydration, avoid standing directly in sunlight, and to take breaks in the shade. Despite most of the buses being idle, the air in '''Level 411''' is often polluted due to frequent internal combustion of engines in running buses.

== Description ==
'''Level 411''' splits into 3 notable areas: The Bus Park, The Maintenance Hall, and The Terminal. 

=== The Bus Park ===
{{Blurimage|file=Levelbusbusbusbus209sandbox.jpg|caption=A section of the Bus Park in Level 411.}}
The Bus Park is considered the main area of '''Level 411'''. This area is where wanderers will find the most amount of buses in '''Level 411''' compared to the other areas. Roads in the depot are usually marked with clear lane lines, arrows, and sometimes numbered bays. The surface appears to be made out of concrete or asphalt. In certain areas, there are also turning circles or open maneuvering areas at the ends of rows to help buses change direction smoothly. On either side of the lanes, there are designated parking bays where buses are parked in neat rows, either angled or parallel depending on the depot’s design. Each bay is spaced widely to allow enough room for buses to enter, exit, and open doors without obstruction. Sometimes, buses can be found parked outside of a designated parking area, blocking the depot's road. In certain areas of the depot, empty parking areas can be found with little to no buses parked.
{{Blurimage|file=5thsandboximagefor209test.jpg|caption=Another image of Level 411 with irregularly parked buses.}}
The buses themselves all have different liveries in different sections of the bus park. Some may be identical, while others are entirely different. There are also a wide variety of buses that can be found in '''Level 411''', most notably single-decker passenger buses. Each bus will have a small button near the front door and pressing it will cause the doors to open. However, some buses may not have that button, in which wanderers will have to break down the front door for access into the bus. Inside the bus itself, there are rows of seats on both sides of the central aisle, handrails and poles, large windows, and a driver's cabin. Wanderers can sometimes find [[Object List|items]] scattered on the seats of the bus. The double-decker buses also has stairs leading to the upper section, where more passenger seats can be found.
{{Blurimage|file=Busbusbus209sandboxforlevel.jpg|caption=An image of buses in Level 411, featuring double-decker buses.}}
An unusual thing in '''Level 411''' is that while the engine can be started in most buses, they are unable to be driven even while shifting the gear. This is not the case for all buses however as rarely, one can find a drivable bus. While buses in '''Level 411''' can be damaged and tampered with, some buses appear to regenerate themselves after a certain period of time. It is also unknown if the bus itself can be driven manually out of '''Level 411'''. In some sections of the depot, buses may not be parked spaciously enough for one to walk through, and can be cramped and squeezed among other buses, usually leaving a gap of not more than 20-30cm in between. It is also heavily unadvised to stand near the tailpipe of a bus that runs on internal combustion engines, as these have been known to secrete harmful gases which can severely damage the body, with the most common being gaseous carbon compounds, and other forms of unburnt hydrocarbons. Additionally, some rare buses have been known to send wanderers to other levels by simply entering the bus.

=== The Maintenance Hall ===
{{Blurimage|file=Warehousehallltestimage209.jpg|caption=An image of a Maintenance Hall in Level 411.}}
The Maintenance Hall, alternatively called the Garage, the Facility, or Storage Hall, can appear as a large warehouse on the outside, with each maintenance hall appearing every 5km away from each other throughout the bus park. Inside the maintenance hall, repair equipment such as wrenches and screwdrivers can be found here, along with some buses that have broken down. Posters of scheduled bus departure timings can also be found here, although they serve no purpose as there have been no cases of someone driving a bus out of '''Level 411'''. Tires and scrap metal are also commonly found in this area of '''Level 411'''. While most buses of '''Level 411''' are parked outdoors, some can be found in indoor garages of the warehouse, It is also advised for wanderers to enter this area when the hot and humid weather starts to severely impact the body, as many supplies can be found here including food, paper, toilets, water dispensers, and even a kitchen in some cases. Lights and fans here also appear to be powered endlessly with no switches to turn them off. The buses that have broken down appear to be scheduled for repair, though no staff have ever been observed performing repairs as such. On certain occasions, the engines of the buses here may start on their own before shutting down, and headlights may flicker or turn on entirely.

{{Blurimage|file=Level209busbusbus2sandbox.jpg|caption=Buses in Level 411 parked in a sheltered garage of a maintenance hall.}}

The Maintenance Hall also has offices that are usually meant for administration, records, and staff operations. Most of the offices contains desks, filing cabinets, computer monitors, route maps, paperwork, and locked drawers. Papers may have bus routes, employee names, or unknown logs written on it. Occasionally, CCTV monitors show live footage from areas that are not physically nearby, or may blast static and distortions through the monitor. Smaller storage rooms can be found with more boxes and shelves full of supplies or simply a janitor's closet. Ventilation shafts can also be seen near the ceiling, removing any air-pollutants produced by the buses found in the hall. However, some wanderers have reported faint noises coming from within these shafts, which are described as knocking, dragging, or occasionally what resembles a muffled voice. Investigation is not recommended, as the shafts are too narrow to safely navigate. Wanderers who remain in the hall for extended periods have noted subtle changes in the environment. Upon further analysis, the posters of departures may display unfamiliar routes or destinations that do not exist elsewhere in the bus park. Supplies found in the storage rooms also replenish every hour, though it is unknown how this happens. Despite its safety compared to other areas, the Maintenance Hall is not devoid of risk. Prolonged stays have been associated with auditory hallucinations and a growing sense of paranoia.

=== The Terminal ===
Each Maintenance Hall will have an adjacent bus terminal. The terminal can be accessed through following signs to exits in the Maintenance Hall that leads here. In this section of '''Level 411''', it is regarded as quite possibly the most anomalous area. Even though buses cannot be driven out manually from the depot, it appears that in this section, there are self-driving buses seen entering the terminal from unknown roads blocked by tall trees and bushes. There are also waiting areas and bus berths with long rows of benches and seats. In every terminal, there are usually five boarding zones meant to cater to ten bus services, though buses rarely pick up passengers or wanderers waiting for the bus. In each boarding berth, there are two signs meant to display text, but most signs are either empty or damaged.

Beyond the terminal, behind the trees and bushes, lies an empty road that cannot be accessed through normal means. Attempting to walk out of the terminal's exit into the highway will cause one to be brought into a random Maintenance Hall, away from the area they once were. Buses can sometimes be seen driving along the road, though they may travel without drivers, headlights, or passengers. However, in certain terminals, '''Level 411''' can schedule buses with the service number [[Level 11|11]] or [[Level 69|69]].

== Entities ==
Rare but present, [[Entity 9|Facelings]], [[Phenomenon 35|Wretches]], and [[Entity 23|Carbonations]] can be found in '''Level 411''', where most cases were spotted in the Bus Park. The buses have also been suspected to be entities, though it remains unconfirmed.

== Communities, Bases, and Outposts ==
While it is highly difficult to set up an outpost in '''Level 411''', there have been successful reports and documented groups.

=== The Bus Scavengers ===

* Less than 100 members.
* Willing to trade.
* Accepts and recruits lost wanderers.
* Found in the Bus Park.
* Focused on looting buses within '''Level 411'''.
* Strips damaged or unusable buses for fuel, spare parts, and raw materials.

=== Wanderers Of The Depot ===

* Approximately 64 members, most which are formerly cleaners, mechanics, and drivers.
* Found in a Maintenance Hall.
* Also known as the “Children of the Depot”.
* Extremely territorial and defensive; respond violently to theft, sabotage, or damage to the depot.
* Focus heavily on maintenance, reinforcements, and improving living conditions within '''Level 411'''.
Despite the name, they are far from being isolated wanderers. It is said that the Wanderers Of The Depot have also been trying to make buses in The Terminal of good use, by studying and attempting to find out how '''Level 411''' starts self-driving buses in the terminal, and if they can extract, harness, or replicate these anomalies to create transport systems linking to other levels. But so far all attempts have been unsuccessful.

More communities have yet to be found.

== Entrances and Exits ==

=== Entrances ===

* Finding and entering a bus in [[Level 158]] will bring one here.
* Finding and noclipping into a metallic structure in [[Level 242]] will bring one here.
* Entering brown double-doors in [[Level 223]] will bring one here.
* Accidentally tripping or falling into a shopping cart in [[Level 197]] will lead one here.
* It is also rumored that one can enter '''Level 411''' from [[Level 179]], though this is unconfirmed and should not be attempted as the latter is considered a dead end.

Additionally, boarding a bus in [[Level 11]] displaying "Service 11, towards L411 Bus Depot" which departed from '''Level 411''' will transport one here in approximately 45-60 minutes.

=== Exits ===

* Finding a blue bus with blue windows, blue rims and blue tires parked in the Bus Park and entering it will send one to [[Level 280]].
* Finding a neon-themed bus parked in the Bus Park and entering it will send one to [[Level 154]].
* Finding a white, snow-themed bus with an advertisement for a pair of skis in the Bus Park and entering it will send one to [[Level 210]].
* Climbing to the ventilation shaft in the Maintenance Hall and entering it will bring one to [[Level 297]].
* Crashing a drivable bus into another drivable bus in the Bus Park will cause the driver to be send to [[Level 488]]. This will not work if a drivable bus is crashed into a dormant, undrivable bus.

Rarely, terminals may display [[Level 11|two]] [[Level 69|services]] for departure. These buses will depart from the boarding zone, out of the terminal and into the empty road behind the bushes, where the wanderer will not be taken to another Maintenance Hall this time so long as they are in the departing bus. 

The services that '''Level 411''' provides will either display, "Service 11, towards L411 Bus Depot, looping trip via [[Level 11|Endless City]]" or, "Service 69, towards [[Level 69|Hilbert's Motel]], terminating trip".

* Boarding Service 11 will transport one to [[Level 11]] in 45-60 minutes. Wanderers are not forced to leave when the bus arrives at [[Level 11]] as it serves as a looping point for the bus to drive back to '''Level 411'''.
* Boarding Service 69 will transport one to [[Level 69]] in 60-85 minutes. This time, wanderers will be forced to leave the bus as [[Level 69]] serves  as a terminating point for that servcie. If a wanderer refuses to leave the bus in 5 minutes, they will be forcefully kicked out by an unknown force, and the bus will then drive away.

There are no other services generated by '''Level 411'''. However the Wanderers Of The Depot are attempting to manually forge new services with extensive research and studies.
